summary: Russian forces conducted kinetic intercepts of Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs using "Elka" and "Yolka" air defense drones in spring 2026, while the "Rubicon" Center reported 132 enemy targets destroyed on March 13 and 111 on March 12, including FPV anti-drone operations and strikes on Ukrainian UAV control points in Donbas, Sumy, and Krasny Liman directions. Ukraine received two Phantom MK-1 humanoid robots for testing, described as part of a "full-scale robot war" by their co-founder. Ukrainian FPV tactics included targeting Russian infantry on horseback and using decoy drones to counter Russian defenses, while Russian forces destroyed enemy unmanned boats in the Dnipro River mouth and Tendrov Spit area using FPV drones.
